title: What is Depth Estimation?
concept: Depth estimation predicts how far every point in a scene is from the camera, converting a flat 2D image into a 3D understanding of distance — essential for robotics, AR, and autonomous navigation.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why does raw accuracy fail as an evaluation metric for rare-anomaly detection problems?

options.text: A model that flags nothing can still score very high accuracy while catching zero real anomalies
Accuracy cannot be computed for anomaly detection tasks
Anomaly detectors never produce measurable predictions

exercises.explain: Yes! Extreme rarity makes raw accuracy misleadingly high even for useless models.

cards.title: A photo has no distance built in
cards.body: Look at any regular photo: a mountain in the background and a coffee cup in the foreground might occupy similarly-sized regions of the image, even though one is a mile away and the other is inches from the lens. A flat photo alone doesn't tell you 'how far'.

cards.title: Depth Estimation
cards.body: Depth estimation predicts, for every pixel in an image, its distance from the camera — producing a depth map where each pixel value represents distance instead of color, turning a flat 2D image into a 3D understanding of the scene.

cards.title: X-ray vision for distance
cards.body: Regular vision tells you WHAT is in a scene (colors, shapes, objects). Depth estimation adds a layer of 'X-ray vision' that tells you HOW FAR each part of the scene is — like overlaying an invisible ruler across every point in the photo.

cards.title: Robot navigation
cards.body: A warehouse robot uses depth estimation to know that a shelf is 2 meters ahead and a box is 0.5 meters to the left, letting it plan a safe path around obstacles — information a plain 2D color image alone cannot directly provide.

cards.title: Depth maps aren't literally 'seeing through' objects
cards.body: It's a myth that depth estimation gives X-ray vision through solid objects. It only estimates the distance to the NEAREST visible surface at each pixel — exactly what a camera can see, not what's hidden behind it.

left.text:
Encodes color/brightness per pixel
Answers: 'What does this look like?'
No distance information

right.text:
Encodes distance per pixel
Answers: 'How far away is this?'
Enables 3D scene understanding

exercises.prompt: What does a depth map encode at each pixel?

options.text: The distance of that point in the scene from the camera
The color value of that pixel
The exact object class label at that pixel
The compression level of the image file

exercises.explain: Correct! A depth map encodes per-pixel distance from the camera.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Depth estimation lets a camera see through solid objects to what's hidden behind them.
exercises.explain: False. It only estimates distance to the nearest visible surface, not what's hidden behind objects.

exercises.sentence: Depth estimation turns a flat 2D image into a ___ understanding of the scene.
exercises.explain: Depth adds the missing dimension needed for 3D scene understanding.

exercises.prompt: Why can't a plain 2D color photo alone tell you that a distant mountain and a nearby coffee cup are at very different distances?

options.text: Color and shape alone don't encode distance; two objects can appear similarly sized in the frame despite being at very different real-world distances
Mountains and coffee cups always appear the same color in photos
2D photos cannot capture any objects larger than 1 meter

exercises.explain: Correct! Perceived size in a flat photo doesn't reliably reveal true distance without depth information.

exercises.prompt: A drone needs to know precisely how far it is from a wall to avoid a collision, using only its onboard camera. Which capability does it need?

options.text: Depth estimation, to convert the 2D camera image into per-pixel distance information for obstacle avoidance
Image classification alone, since knowing the object's category is sufficient for collision avoidance
Style transfer, to make the wall look more visually appealing
None of these; distance cannot be estimated from any camera image

exercises.explain: Exactly! Depth estimation directly provides the distance information needed for safe navigation. Next: the classical two-camera approach — stereo depth estimation.
